headline: Estee Lauder and IMAX Higher; Ross Stores Boost Forecast | Stock Movers
summary: On this episode of Stock Movers with Alexis Christoforous: - Estee Lauder (EL) share are climbing following collapse of a proposed combination with Puig Brands that would have created one of the world's largest fragrance and skincare companies. - IMAX Corp. (IMAX) shares jumped after the Wall Street Journal reported the large-screen theater company is exploring a sale and has approached entertainment companies as potential buyers. - Ross Stores (ROST) shares are up after the off-price retailer boosted its comparable sales forecast for the full year. The firm also posted better-than-expected sales for the first quarter.

content.title: Estee Lauder and IMAX Higher; Ross Stores Boost Forecast | Stock Movers
content.description:
On this episode of Stock Movers with Alexis Christoforous:

- Estee Lauder (EL) share are climbing following collapse of a proposed combination with Puig Brands that would have created one of the world's largest fragrance and skincare companies. 

- IMAX Corp. (IMAX) shares jumped after the Wall Street Journal reported the large-screen theater company is exploring a sale and has approached entertainment companies as potential buyers.

- Ross Stores (ROST) shares are up after the off-price retailer boosted its comparable sales forecast for the full year. The firm also posted better-than-expected sales for the first quarter.
content.summary: On this episode of Stock Movers with Alexis Christoforous: - Estee Lauder (EL) share are climbing following collapse of a proposed combination with Puig Brands that would have created one of the world's largest fragrance and skincare companies. - IMAX Corp. (IMAX) shares jumped after the Wall Street Journal reported the large-screen theater company is exploring a sale and has approached entertainment companies as potential buyers. - Ross Stores (ROST) shares are up after the off-price retailer boosted its comparable sales forecast for the full year. The firm also posted better-than-expected sales for the first quarter.

headline: Wall Street rises toward another winning week, even as U.S. households feel more discouraged
summary: The split between Wall Street and most U.S. households grew even wider Friday, as U.S. stocks rose toward the finish of an eighth straight winning week, their longest such streak since 2023.  The S&P 500 added 0.6% and pulled closer to its all-time high set in the middle of last week.

content.title: Wall Street rises toward another winning week, even as U.S. households feel more discouraged
content.summary: The split between Wall Street and most U.S. households grew even wider Friday, as U.S. stocks rose toward the finish of an eighth straight winning week, their longest such streak since 2023.  The S&P 500 added 0.6% and pulled closer to its all-time high set in the middle of last week.

thumbnail.caption: Specialist Anthony Matesic, left, and trader Fred Demarco work on the floor of the New York Stock Exchange, Friday, May 22, 2026. (AP Photo/Richard Drew)

headline: Wall Street keeps rising, even as U.S. households keep getting more discouraged
summary: The split between Wall Street and most U.S. households grew wider Friday, as U.S. stocks rose toward the finish of an eighth straight winning week, their best such streak since 2023.  The S&P 500 added 0.8% and pulled closer to its all-time high set in the middle of last week.  Ross Stores helped drive the market and rose 6.9% after the off-price retailer reported profit and revenue for the latest quarter that easily cleared analysts’ expectations.

content.title: Wall Street keeps rising, even as U.S. households keep getting more discouraged
content.summary: The split between Wall Street and most U.S. households grew wider Friday, as U.S. stocks rose toward the finish of an eighth straight winning week, their best such streak since 2023.  The S&P 500 added 0.8% and pulled closer to its all-time high set in the middle of last week.  Ross Stores helped drive the market and rose 6.9% after the off-price retailer reported profit and revenue for the latest quarter that easily cleared analysts’ expectations.

thumbnail.caption: Specialist Anthony Matesic, left, and trader Fred Demarco work on the floor of the New York Stock Exchange, Friday, May 22, 2026. (AP Photo/Richard Drew)
